frameworks.name: disinhibition-based circuit motif
frameworks.one_line: local disinhibition gates a hybrid circuit between normalization and WTA regimes

frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: recurrent gain control implementing relative value coding within the hybrid motif

frameworks.name: winner-take-all attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: recurrent excitation with pooled inhibition yielding categorical selection

frameworks.name: working memory persistent activity
frameworks.one_line: circuit produces sustained activity consistent with mnemonic maintenance

core_question: Can a single biologically plausible circuit unify normalized value coding and winner-take-all selection under flexible top-down control?
key_assumptions: normalization and WTA reflect distinct but coexisting circuit motifs
local disinhibition can flexibly gate recurrent dynamics
psychometric and chronometric data constrain decision-circuit models
manipulations: circuit state varied via local disinhibition
task context varied to elicit diverse neural dynamics
comparison of normalization-only vs WTA-only vs hybrid architectures
key_findings: hybrid motif unifies normalized representation and WTA competition
model captures empirical psychometric and chronometric data
produces persistent activity consistent with working memory
interpretations: local disinhibition is a key mechanism for flexible top-down control of decision circuits
disinhibition offers a biologically plausible substrate for task-dependent neural dynamics
